item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image could not be sourced, assumed non-compliant, and the second image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda want to see what happens if you extract/infuse with this then sit the subject down at SCP-434.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 5 bucks they use this thing during 110-Montauk. It extracts the sin from the demon baby, but they have to do it every 24 hours because it generates more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I changed Attention Deficit Disorder to Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder. With the recent DSM update, ADD has been absorbed by ADHD. Even if this were not the case, the symptoms described in this article - increased energy levels, activity and enthusiasm; hyperactivity - would indicate a diagnosis of ADHD rather than ADD under the old DSM.
I subsequently removed "hyperactivity and" as this is a symptom often characteristic of ADHD.

Edit: Upon further reflection I've reverted my edit as it is a content change. I would still recommend that ADD be changed to ADHD as per the updated DSM, but that's up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How would that add to the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want an 8th that is redacted :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really subtle at all, to the point that I recognized the sins halfway through the first one (though to be fair, I do play Binding of Isaac, so the sins may be more familiar to me than to others).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be an -EX, not an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1113-6 extraction would be nice for college students. Think about how much MCD would sell this for. Perfect children, perfect wife, perfect servants!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I grew up catholic and didn't get the deadly sins thing till I read the comments, I feel silly…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite a good piece, and nicely entwined with the 7 deadly sins mythos. Could be an interesting MCD item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I strongly dislike the simplification of human behavior in such a way as "this shiz makes u jelly, yo. this shiz makes u smexy, yo. this shiz makes u foam, breh"

if it had gone into specific physiological detail, like saying that the red fluids contain traces or copious amounts of testosterone or adrenaline, stuff related to actually inducing aggression in the human brain, it would have been significantly better and utterly amazing to think about—being able to extract and/or infuse people with chemicals and hormones naturally occurring without the use of pharmashweaticles. Instead this comes across as more like "the four humours" type magic explanation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's a really good one. I can imagine a researcher sitting at a desk getting really annoyed while trying to think of a way to write a file, while not mentioning any sins. I also wonder what would happen if it was tested on infants… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it—simple and sweet. (Also, being an atheist, the "sin" thing didn't really register with me until I was nearly done with the article and remembered the title ^^; ) Not the strongest article I've ever read, but it's still one of the many good ones I've read since coming back to this site, so +1.

EDIT: Ooh… I just noticed that the vials really ARE filled with strips of paper… heh, yikes ^^; maybe I can find a place to get some test tubes and food coloring and TAKE you a better picture for this. I already took a picture for one of the more well-known articles on the site, so I suppose it's worth a try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe the extraction effect of 1133-4 is intending to say "Similar to SCP-1133-2", not "Similar to SCP-1133-3". I think that's minor enough for me to edit, but ryantherebel is editing now so I'll just say it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, this is a good post, and yes it is excellently made, but the pictures of SPC-1133-x vials are filled with… Strips of paper… I'm sorry, I might even vote up the page if I feel like it, but I hate that picture. Can anyone find anything better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad, I upvoted it. It was quite original, and I liked its concept.

I would like to see an experiment log in which one subject had large and equal amounts of all seven substances drained out of their body, another subject had the same amount of each substance infused within their body, and another subject had amounts of all seven extracted and infused in varying amounts until a perfect balance of vice and virtue was reached. With all three having unexpected results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't it seem like it could cure the negative side effects of that 'evil thoughts' book or that weird sin table thing? it would be interesting to see cross-SCP experiments to test its effects like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One thing I noticed

Ingesting SCP-1133-X into the human body has a similar effect to infusion of the same variety, although lasting for only 2-4 hours, rather than being permanent, although the effect will often manifest immediately.

That seems to be worded a bit clumsily, may I suggest: "A human ingesting SCP-1133-X undergoes a similar process as infusion of the same variety, although lasting for only 2-4 hours rather than being permanent; however, the effect will often manifest immediately."

Also, I don't see a huge amount of change besides it being an automatic switch-out-for-random-other-sin stuff… Not that I'm saying it's a BAD choice, I like it either way, just saying it doesn't seem to be significantly different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been working on a second draft to try to fix the various issues people have highlighted (thanks for that, by the way), and here it is: http://scpsandboxwiki.wikidot.com/falling-piano-s-sandbox

Quite a lot has changed, so I'm going to try to get some feedback before I post this version on the main site in case I managed to make it worse. Kudos to Adam Henderson for suggesting the new take on it, although I'll happily take all the blame if it turns out it hasn't worked. Has it improved?

(also, I'm working on getting rid of that elbow from the picture)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't keeping up on it in the sandbox, but I remember being kind of "meh" on the idea when it was originally posted.
You've certainly caught my interest now.
I also think we need to hook up a few D's to some of 1133-1, and thus facilitate the daily SCP-871 disposal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd prefer it if the individual colour reports were individual spoilers or nested

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was great. Cool idea and I totally caught the 7 sins thing even though I am an atheist myself. Very well written too! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The shoulder in the photo puts me off for some reason, just seems unprofessional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha. Growing up atheist, I didn't catch that reference at all. I thought this was just some odd emotion harvester thing. Not sure a Biblical reference is really a default downvote, though. Whatever or whoever made it could have done that on purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way too stock for me to be comfortable with. It adds or remove tendencies to the 7 sins, but in essence it's just a thing what changes your personality, and not even in an unpredictable or weird way. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Father, forgive me, I.. poked a badger with a spoon!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I realized this was just a sin extractor, I became very disappointed. For what it is, it's set up well, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generally, trust the rating as much as anything. This is at the moment in the positives (+8 as of now). Even though Reject and I both downvoted it.

Remember these from the Rules of Thumb:

  • Bib's Rule of Higher Numbers: The more an article is liked/upvoted, the less weight a single criticism has. No article is universally liked; resist the urge to run off and make {#random_change} because of one person's comments, no matter who said it.
  • Separation Relation: As an SCP becomes more similar to an existing article, the likelihood of it receiving upvotes approaches zero.
    • Bib's Addendum: A single (or few) similarities between your SCP and another are not dealbreakers, provided the differences are substantial enough.
  • Rule of Rule-Breaking: Articles can break any of these rules, as long as your audience collectively enjoys the end result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's possibly worse. Death ain't scary, yo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I don't know what to think… I'll work on it some more this weekend, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought this would be good, but the execution was lame enough that I downvoted. Differing opinions, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We have a bunch of SCPs that combined could do this, but honestly I like it well enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought this would be lame, but the execution is good enough that I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough. Do you think it would be worth splitting pride into hubris and vanity? I'd heard of vanity before, only as 'vainglory', and I thought as a modern invention it might make more sense to run with the modern interpretation of sin.

Also, do you think making it into an item that exchanged one sin for another would make it less redundant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We already have a couple of things like this. The Board Room (I don't recall the number off the top of my head) and the Peri Logismon, which, incidentally, has the historically accurate eighth "evil thought" (predecessor to "deadly sin" in Catholic religious tradition, circa 4th century) - pride split into two, hubris and vanity.

Decently written, but redundant, so, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not keen on the idea of an 8th one. 567 does a similar thing and I really don't think I could do it better.

I think the idea of exchanging one sin for another in equal amounts is great, though. It would make it much less straightforward and would give a better reason for people actually wanting to use it. Do people think that would be worth implementing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this one coming a mile away and I still liked it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so cheesy it gives me shits.

You don't even hint at an effect, you offload all of the work onto the reader.

As an aside, I wonder whether the eighth thing being original sin would do any good to this. Probably not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe just a "loads of deaths" style thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. I don't like such expungements of that nature. It'd be better if one gave hints as to what it was, or what it did. A flat out expungement doesn't tell us anything and it weighs an article down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, as I find this a creepy little idea. Good.

Now, here's my idea for a twist which would make this much creeper, and provide the "hook" people keep complaining about:

You can use the device to "switch" the sins for equal quantities of each different type of sin. And there are eight types not just seven.

Wrath

Greed

Envy

Pride

Gluttony

Sloth

Lust

[DATA EXPUNGED]

All of these apart from [DATA EXPUNGED] can be extracted from normal people and transferred. [DATA EXPUNGED] can only be created by means of the "switching" function of this device. What happens when it is injected into a Class-D is well… ….[EXPUNGED]. But the foundation fears it to the point where they re-rated this as something other than "Safe."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A single nitpick.

SCP-1133-1 are unable to resist the compulsion to consume any form of food in the immediate area, including those normally repulsive to humans, including rotten food, raw meat and animal feed.

Repeat of 'including' here.

Other than that, interesting but no real hook, just a straight '7 deadly sins'. No vote, as I got bored when I realized what it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My my, we've had quite a good number of quality first SCPs as of late. You've earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you mean. Well, at least it doesn't seem so dreadful that I won't have time to work on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel a bit slow now for not getting the 7 Deadly Sins connection right away. All the same, great idea; +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A machine to change the sins and behaviours of others. It seems pretty cool, and I like the picture of the vials. 1-up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check out SCP-567. It deals with a similar concept of "sins", but changes up the sinful actions enough that it's more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it on the sandbox and I love it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. I'll work on making it a bit more subtle. Should probably start by changing the title to make it less obvious - any suggestions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Far too straightforward of a 7 Deadly Sins interpretation.

I do like the infuse/extract thing, though. Maybe if you just changed what the substances into something less obviously the Sins. Downvote for the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brown/blue and yellow/dark green seem to have similar effects. Other than those I thought it was very well written and original. +1

Edit—
Sins went clear over my head, but it's still an original method of implementing them. I think.
